text.title: Custom Guardrails Pipeline Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for the custom Python guardrails module.

rule.statement: The pipeline is composed of exactly four single-purpose components: PIIDetector, PromptInjectionDetector, ContentModerator, HallucinationDetector. Each is independently testable and independently disable-able via config flag.

rationale.source: Internal architecture review — guardrails maintainability audits

rule.rationale: Monolithic guardrail modules force regression of unrelated checks on every change. Single-purpose components let you swap one (e.g., replace OpenAI moderation with local Llama Guard) without touching the others.

rule.statement: Tier 1 = compiled regex patterns only (length cap + injection signatures). No imports beyond `re`. Must complete in under 1 ms p99.

rationale.source: Guardrails AI perf docs; Cloudflare WAF SRE notes

rule.rationale: Tier 1 runs on 100% of requests. Any dependency that adds > 1 ms blows the median latency budget. Compile patterns once at import time; do not recompile per request.

rule.statement: Independent checks (PII detection + injection detection + moderation) run concurrently via `asyncio.gather`. Only checks that depend on previous output (hallucination detector depends on `process_output` input) run sequentially.

rationale.source: Python asyncio docs; internal latency benchmarks

rule.rationale: Sequential execution stacks tier-2 + tier-3 latency linearly. Concurrent execution caps total latency at max(checks) instead of sum(checks); cuts input processing by ~40%.

rule.statement: `process_input` returns `(Optional[str], list[dict])`. `None` in position 0 means BLOCK the request — the LLM must not be called. Any non-None string means proceed with that sanitised input.

rationale.source: Python typing PEP 484; internal API contracts

rule.rationale: The None-means-block convention makes the blocking decision explicit and type-checker enforceable. Truthy-string convention is ambiguous (empty string vs filtered out).

rule.statement: The public entry point `run()` returns a `GuardrailResult` dataclass with `is_safe`, `input_modified`, `output_modified`, `original_input`, `sanitized_input`, `original_output`, `filtered_output`, `violations`, `metadata`. Never `dict[str, Any]`.
rule.rationale: Untyped dicts let callers silently drift on field names; typed result gives the IDE, type checker, and downstream audit log a single shape to reason about.

rule.statement: On any tier-1 fail with severity high+ (injection match, moderation flag), short-circuit: return early without running tier-2/3. On tier-2/3 fail, populate `violations[]` but continue (downstream `is_safe` flag decides blocking).
rule.rationale: Short-circuiting on critical Tier-1 hits cuts the worst-case latency for known-bad inputs from ~600 ms (full pipeline) to under 5 ms; turns the pipeline into a DDoS-resistant gate.
